role_summary: Civil Rights History Project interviewee; the LoC oral history is the primary archival source for this catalog page.
ai_reading: Joseph Echols Lowery's top semantic neighbor is C. T. Vivian (entry 134, count 131); the broader cluster includes Charles McLaurin (#17), Wyatt Tee Walker (#132), Robert L. Carter (#108). The catalog flags a +0.78 reading toward Armed Self-Defense on the Nonviolence as Theology vs Armed Self-Defense axis as the most-distinctive structural reading.
biographical_paragraph: Joseph Echols Lowery's oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 66 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. The LoC transcript carries a publication-block audit tier: known issues exist that prevent full publication-grade certification, and any specific biographical or quoted claims drawn from the transcript itself should be confirmed against external sources before citation. Independent biographical sourcing for an extended orientation paragraph is pending; the strongest signal currently available is the embedding pattern named in the AI's reading above.
